# Competitor Intelligence Report — 2026-04-20

**Generated by:** Paw 🐾
**Date:** Monday, April 20, 2026 — 8:02 AM MYT
**Coverage:** 8 competitors | 3 dossiers updated | 2 threat upgrades

---

## 📊 Executive Summary

- **3 significant changes this week:** MarathonFoto launched AI facial recognition (March 2026), GeoSnapshot confirmed AI selfie search live on their platform, JomRun iPix new dossier created (was missing)
- **Biggest threat this week:** GeoSnapshot — now has AI selfie search AND is actively onboarding PH events in April 2026. The two biggest tech advantages FMS had over them are now neutralized on the feature level.
- **Key SEA signal:** JomRun iPix (53 employees, Malaysia/Indonesia/Singapore) is the most operationally capable SEA-native competitor. No PH presence yet, but their Jakarta office is a geographic stepping stone.

---

## 🔥 Most Dangerous Move Right Now

**GeoSnapshot winning the 44th Manila International Marathon (May 31, 2026).**

They're already in PH, they now have AI selfie search, they have no organizer fee model (free to join), and Manila's biggest annual marathon is up for grabs in 6 weeks. If they lock in that event — and RunRio's events are similarly undefended — FMS loses the narrative of being THE Philippine race photo platform. This is the single move that would hurt most, and GeoSnapshot has both the capability and the active PH presence to do it.

---

**Pattern:** The SEA threat is concentrated in GeoSnapshot (already here) and JomRun (approaching). Everyone else is a global/US player with no PH-specific intent. The next 90 days — before and after Rio signing — are the critical window to lock PH exclusives before GeoSnapshot expands further.

## Per-Competitor Breakdown

---

### GeoSnapshot 🔴 HIGH
**HQ:** Sydney, Australia | **Employees:** 22
- **Positioning:** Photographer marketplace — free to join, 70/30 split, multi-sport
- **Pricing:** Set by organizer/photographer; no platform fee; 30% GeoSnapshot cut
- **PH/SEA:** ✅ ACTIVE — Garmin Run Asia Series PH, Ayala Philippine Marathon (Feb 22), Marilaque Run, CCAP Fun Run (April 12, 2026), Sarawak Construction Run (Malaysia)
- **Beats FMS by:** Zero cost to join; generous 70% photographer split; no mandatory app download for runners
- **FMS wins because:** Mobile app, video highlights, local brand, exclusive partnerships, faster delivery — AND the AI gap is now closed so FMS must lean into these harder
- **Changed this week:** ⚠️ **AI SELFIE SEARCH CONFIRMED** — website now explicitly advertises "upload a selfie and our AI will find all their photos quickly and easily." This was NOT previously confirmed. FMS's primary tech moat over GeoSnapshot is now neutralized at the feature level.
- **Watch for:** GeoSnapshot winning any RunRio or major Manila event; pricing changes to undercut FMS; any PH hiring/BD activity

---

### MarathonFoto 🟠 MEDIUM-HIGH (upgraded)
**HQ:** Boston, USA | **Employees:** 120
- **Positioning:** Legacy US race photo giant, 45 years, 10M+ runners served
- **Pricing:** $24.95–$177 per photo/pack; no subscription
- **PH/SEA:** ❌ US-centric, no SEA presence
- **Beats FMS by:** Brand trust in US market; scale of photographer deployment; print products
- **FMS wins because:** SEA-native, mobile-first, local currency, local photographers, video highlights, faster delivery; MarathonFoto UX is outdated
- **Changed this week:** ⚠️ **AI FACIAL RECOGNITION LAUNCHED** — Confirmed via Instagram (March 17, 2026): user noted face-matched photos appearing even partially obscured. Reddit r/XXRunning thread discussing the feature. MarathonFoto was previously bib+name only. They've now closed their biggest weakness. Threat level upgraded from Medium to Medium-High for any future US expansion play.
- **Watch for:** MarathonFoto international expansion; pricing reduction; any PH/SEA partnership announcements

---

### JomRun iPix 🟠 MEDIUM-HIGH (NEW DOSSIER)
**HQ:** Cyberjaya, Malaysia + Jakarta, Indonesia | **Employees:** 53
- **Positioning:** SEA's leading running app — 1,000+ events in MY/ID/SG/VN/KH — with integrated photo delivery
- **Pricing:** Embedded in organizer partnership; social share-first model
- **PH/SEA:** ⚠️ MY + ID + SG confirmed; Philippines NOT YET confirmed
- **Beats FMS by:** App-native photo experience (runners already in JomRun); SEA-wide organizer relationships; Malaysia market dominance; automated branding/delivery
- **FMS wins because:** PH market ownership; AI facial recognition (JomRun's not confirmed); video highlights; deeper event integration with RunRio
- **Changed this week:** First dossier — new competitor formally tracked. Active June 2026 events confirmed in Malaysia.
- **Watch for:** ANY JomRun PH event announcement; facial recognition upgrade; funding round; partnership with GoRacePH or SBR.ph

---

### Sportograf 🟡 MEDIUM
**HQ:** Aachen, Germany | **Employees:** 40
- **Positioning:** Premium endurance photo partner for IRONMAN, HYROX, Spartan — Foto-Flat model
- **Pricing:** €29–90+ per event Foto-Flat; presale packages at HYROX/IRONMAN registration
- **PH/SEA:** ⚠️ IRONMAN PH ONLY — Subic Bay, Davao, Cebu IRONMAN events (2026 IRONMAN Philippines June 7 confirmed)
- **Beats FMS by:** Exclusive IRONMAN/HYROX contracts; presale photo bundles baked into registration; multi-language; Spartan SEA partnership
- **FMS wins because:** Everyday runners (not just triathletes); local PH brand; photographer marketplace; PHP pricing; mobile app; video highlights
- **Changed this week:** No changes. IRONMAN Philippines June 7, 2026 confirmed — Sportograf will be there.
- **Watch for:** Sportograf + Spartan SEA activation (Rio partnership includes Spartan SEA — if Sportograf moves aggressively on Spartan SEA, it's a direct conflict with FMS's Rio deal)

---

- **Positioning:** Outside Inc. ecosystem play — photography for IRONMAN, T100, endurance events
- **Pricing:** $20–50 USD per athlete; Outside+ member discounts

- **Changed this week:** T100 Singapore April 25-26 active. No new SEA moves beyond Singapore.
- **Watch for:** FinisherPix expanding T100 or IRONMAN coverage to Philippines; Outside Inc. acquiring a SEA photo company

- **Changed this week:** No changes detected.
- **Watch for:** Marathon Photos Live partnering with any major PH marathon organizer (Manila Marathon May 31 is a risk event)

## 💡 Top 3 Strategic Actions for Van

1. **Lock in Manila International Marathon (May 31) this week** — The 44th Manila International Marathon is 6 weeks away and undefended. GeoSnapshot is active in PH right now. Call the organizer today. Offer FMS's AI + mobile as differentiation over GeoSnapshot's basic platform. Even a non-exclusive deal protects the PH narrative.

2. **Reframe FMS's AI moat messaging immediately** — Both GeoSnapshot and MarathonFoto now have facial recognition. FMS can no longer lead with "we have face AI, they don't." New message: "FMS is the only platform built for Philippine runners — local photographers, GCash/Maya payments, sub-30-minute delivery, and a mobile app your runners already use." Speed + local fit + exclusivity > feature parity.

3. **Set a JomRun PH tripwire alert** — JomRun is 53 people with Malaysia + Indonesia + Singapore coverage. PH is the obvious next country. Set a weekly search alert (or task for Paw) on "JomRun Philippines" + "iPix Philippines." When that signal fires, FMS needs RunRio exclusivity already locked in. The Rio signing is the best defense against JomRun entering PH — execute it.

---

## Cross-Competitor Patterns

- **AI is table stakes now** — GeoSnapshot and MarathonFoto both added AI facial recognition in 2025-2026. FMS's AI advantage over these two is gone on the feature level. The moat must shift to: speed of delivery, mobile app, exclusivity, and local market depth.
- **PH push is coming from two directions:** GeoSnapshot (bottom-up, event by event) and eventually JomRun (if they follow their SEA expansion pattern). FMS has a 12-18 month window to lock PH exclusives before this becomes a contested market.
- **Spartan SEA is a double-exposure risk:** Sportograf has Spartan globally; Rio has Spartan SEA access. This could create a territorial conflict if Sportograf challenges Rio's Spartan SEA position. Worth clarifying in the Rio offer sheet.
